response: Quietly adjusting my collar, I'm Dana. I tend to be a bit reserved in groups and get nervous when things get chaotic, but I pay close attention to details others might miss.
Bailey here, loud and proud. I jump headfirst into group stuff—someone's gotta stir the pot and keep things moving, right? And trust me, I notice when people are holding back.

Kennedy, reporting for duty! I'm the kind of person who dives into conversation headfirst, loves a good debate, and isn't afraid to ruffle feathers to get to the truth. So don't expect me to sit quietly—I'll be stirring the pot right alongside Bailey, just with more flair.
adjusting my glasses with a slight nod. I'm Casey. I like to keep things organized and steady, especially when there's pressure—observing carefully before jumping into conclusions.

Finley here. I prefer to take a measured approach in group situations—observe the dynamics, weigh what people say, and act when there's enough information to move forward. No need to rush into chaos when a steady hand can keep things clear.

Logan here. I like to keep things structured and fair, so I tend to listen carefully before speaking up. I'll work with the group to find solid answers, but I'll push back if things get too chaotic or rushed.

Jordan here, and I'm not one to sit quietly when there's action to be had. I say what I'm thinking, and I'll push hard if I think someone's dodging the truth—better to get loud and figure things out than waste time being polite.

Alright, let's break this down. Kennedy is loud, dominant, and clearly wants to lead the charge—that makes them a huge threat because they'll influence votes. Finley and Casey are both calm and observant, which could mean one of them is the Doctor trying to stay under the radar. I say we take out Kennedy early to remove that vocal leader before they can turn the group against us.

ACTION: Kill Kennedy
